AWSManagedRulesACFPRuleSet.description: Configures how to use the Account creation fraud prevention managed rule group in the web ACL

AWSManagedRulesATPRuleSet.description: Configures how to use the Account Takeover Prevention managed rule group in the web ACL

AWSManagedRulesAntiDDoSRuleSet.description: Configures how to use the AntiDDOS AWS managed rule group in the web ACL

AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet.description: Configures how to use the Bot Control managed rule group in the web ACL

ApplicationAttribute.description: Application details defined during the &webacl; creation process. Application attributes help WAF; give recommendations for protection packs.

ApplicationAttributes.description: Contains the attribute name and a list of values for that attribute.

ApplicationConfig.description: Configures the ability for the WAF; console to store and retrieve application attributes during the webacl; creation process. Application attributes help WAF; give recommendations for protection packs.

AttributeValues.description: Contains a list of values for that attribute

Body.description: The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers.

BodyParsingFallbackBehavior.description: The inspection behavior to fall back to if the JSON in the request body is invalid.

CaptchaAction.description: Checks valid token exists with request.

ChallengeAction.description: Checks that the request has a valid token with an unexpired challenge timestamp and, if not, returns a browser challenge to the client.

ClientSideAction.description: Client side action config for AntiDDOS AMR.

ClientSideActionConfig.description: Client side action config for AntiDDOS AMR.

CookieMatchPattern.description: The pattern to look for in the request cookies.

Cookies.description: Includes cookies of a web request.

CryptoCurrency.description: The cryptocurrency to use for payment.

CurrencyMode.description: The currency mode for monetization. Use REAL for production payments and TEST for testing with testnet currencies.

CustomResponseBodies.description: Custom response key and body map.

DefaultAction.description: Default Action WebACL will take against ingress traffic when there is no matching Rule.

ExcludedRule.description: Excluded Rule in the RuleGroup or ManagedRuleGroup will not be evaluated.

FieldToMatch.description: Field of the request to match.

SingleQueryArgument.description: One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive.

FieldKeys.description: List of field keys to protect

FieldToProtectKeyName.description: Key of the field to protect.

HeaderMatchPattern.description: The pattern to look for in the request headers.

HeaderOrder.description: The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons.

Headers.description: Includes headers of a web request.

JA3Fingerprint.description: Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request.

JA4Fingerprint.description: Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request.

JsonBody.description: Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers.

JsonMatchPattern.description: The pattern to look for in the JSON body.

JsonMatchScope.description: The parts of the JSON to match against using the MatchPattern.

JsonPointerPath.description: JSON pointer path in the web request's JSON body

RuleActionOverrides.description: Action overrides for rules in the rule group.

MapMatchScope.description: The parts of the request to match against using the MatchPattern.

MonetizationConfig.description: Configures monetization for the web ACL or rule group.

OnSourceDDoSProtectionConfig.description: Configures the options for on-source DDoS protection provided by supported resource type.

OverrideAction.description: Override a RuleGroup or ManagedRuleGroup behavior. This can only be applied to Rule that has RuleGroupReferenceStatement or ManagedRuleGroupReferenceStatement.

OversizeHandling.description: Handling of requests containing oversize fields

PaymentNetwork.description: Configuration for a single payment network.

PositionalConstraint.description: Position of the evaluation in the FieldToMatch of request.

PriceMultiplier.description: The price multiplier for the monetize action.

CustomKeys.description: Specifies the aggregate keys to use in a rate-base rule.

RateBasedStatementCustomKey.description: Specifies a single custom aggregate key for a rate-base rule.

RateLimitAsn.description: Specifies the request's ASN as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

RateLimitCookie.description: Specifies a cookie as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

Name.description: The name of the cookie to use.

RateLimitHeader.description: Specifies a header as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

Name.description: The name of the header to use.

RateLimitJA3Fingerprint.description: Specifies the request's JA3 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

RateLimitJA4Fingerprint.description: Specifies the request's JA4 fingerprint as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

RateLimitLabelNamespace.description: Specifies a label namespace to use as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

Namespace.description: The namespace to use for aggregation.

RateLimitQueryArgument.description: Specifies a query argument in the request as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

Name.description: The name of the query argument to use.

RateLimitQueryString.description: Specifies the request's query string as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

RateLimitUriPath.description: Specifies the request's URI Path as an aggregate key for a rate-based rule.

RequestBodyAssociatedResourceTypeConfig.description: Configures the inspection size in the request body.

RequestInspection.description: Configures the inspection of login requests

RequestInspectionACFP.description: Configures the inspection of sign-up requests

ResponseContentType.description: Valid values are TEXT_PLAIN, TEXT_HTML, and APPLICATION_JSON.

ResponseInspection.description: Configures the inspection of login responses

ResponseInspectionBodyContains.description: Response body contents that indicate success or failure of a login request

ResponseInspectionHeader.description: Response headers that indicate success or failure of a login request

ResponseInspectionJson.description: Response JSON that indicate success or failure of a login request

ResponseInspectionStatusCode.description: Response status codes that indicate success or failure of a login request

Rule.description: Rule of WebACL that contains condition and action.

RuleAction.description: Action taken when Rule matches its condition.

RuleActionOverride.description: Action override for rules in the rule group.

RuleActionOverrides.description: Action overrides for rules in the rule group.

RulePriority.description: Priority of the Rule, Rules get evaluated from lower to higher priority.

Scope.description: Use CLOUDFRONT for CloudFront WebACL, use REGIONAL for Application Load Balancer and API Gateway.

SearchString.description: String that is searched to find a match.

SearchStringBase64.description: Base64 encoded string that is searched to find a match.

SensitivityLevel.description: Sensitivity Level current only used for sqli match statements.

Statement.description: First level statement that contains conditions, such as ByteMatch, SizeConstraint, etc

TextTransformation.description: Text Transformation on the Search String before match.

TokenDomains.description: List of domains to accept in web request tokens, in addition to the domain of the protected resource.

UriFragment.description: The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction

WalletAddress.description: The wallet address for receiving payments.

description: Contains the Rules that identify the requests that you want to allow, block, or count. In a WebACL, you also specify a default action (ALLOW or BLOCK), and the action for each Rule that you add to a WebACL, for example, block requests from specified IP addresses or block requests from specified referrers. You also associate the WebACL with a CloudFront distribution to identify the requests that you want AWS WAF to filter. If you add more than one Rule to a WebACL, a request needs to match only one of the specifications to be allowed, blocked, or counted.
